purpose: Track small, testable native-port increments without committing ROMs, ASM, extracted assets, or generated proprietary data.

commit_allowed: debug tooling that reads local private data
safe screenshots that contain only original project UI
commit_forbidden: generated roster data derived from the game
tile sheet PNGs derived from the game
audio or other extracted original assets
repo_policy.local_data_boundary: Use --root or TECMO_DECOMP_ROOT to point at a private local decompilation workspace. Keep that workspace outside this repository.

current_runtime.game_core: portable C runtime state plus explicit memory arenas
current_runtime.asset_mode: local development read-only scan/export tools; no committed proprietary data
current_runtime.known_status: prototype launcher/menu, portable controls engine, reusable nametable screen renderer, first native Bank07 fixed-helper C layer, source-backed title screen mode, NBA license screen render target, Bank04 arena third-screen render target with captured background and scoreboard OAM, Play Game progressive intro-output stepper, first-sprite debug proof, bank/table-switchable intro builder lab, CHR playground, roster viewer

local_desktop_shortcut.arguments: --play, with --root added automatically when a local decomp root is discoverable
local_desktop_shortcut.icon_refresh_policy: Every successful build regenerates the original TNB basketball icon and refreshes the Desktop shortcut.
local_desktop_shortcut.icon_policy: Generated original project icon only; do not use copied Tecmo, NBA, ROM, or extracted game artwork.

quick_launch_points.description: Starts directly at the native launcher menu.
quick_launch_points.expected_screen: Launcher menu opens first with Title Screen, Intro Lab, CHR Playground, Play Game, Rosters, Quit.

quick_launch_points.description: Runs the headless runtime input-flow check that reaches rosters, enters the Play Game progressive intro-output mode, returns to menu, and confirms the quit path.
quick_launch_points.expected_output: FLOW TEST PASS: menu rosters play-intro quit

quick_launch_points.description: Runs the native Bank07 fixed-helper C counterpart checks, including the decoded L88E7 CDAC IRQ setup contract, without loading ROM data or starting an emulator.
quick_launch_points.expected_output: BANK07 C HELPER SELF TEST PASS

quick_launch_points.description: Runs the portable controls engine checks for held, pressed, released, and named button mapping without opening a window.
Traces the local-only Bank07 reset/bootstrap scheduler handoff into the first Bank04 intro script target without committing source bytes or asset payloads.
Traces the first Bank04 intro stream record staged through fixed $C051/$D861, including the first visible rabbit record, without committing decoded output.
Renders the runtime boot screen before any launcher input.
Renders the launcher menu to a PNG for screenshot QA.
Renders the launcher menu with debug overlay enabled to a safe committed PNG.
Renders the in-app Title Screen mode. When the ignored composite trace exists, it shows the local rabbit trace plus asset-backed TECMO splash; Enter opens the launcher and Esc quits at runtime.
Renders the NBA Properties license screen from the local Lua palette/attribute capture plus the decoded Bank00 nametable stream rows using the shared native nametable renderer.
Renders the first-sprite debug proof from the local decoded intro trace. Shows the first staged D861 sprite, the first visible rabbit sprite, and the full 16-record/64-byte rabbit stream table.

Renders the Bank04 L88E7 first-intro proof with the local $89DD palette snapshot, the A7DB selector 0 stream, the A7DB selector 1 rabbit stream, the $0100=05 IRQ/vector interpretation, and the Bank00 PRESENTS data lead.

Renders the Bank04 $88E8 third-screen arena proof from the refreshed Lua capture: screen id $18 nametable frames 428-430, palette fade frames 425-476, live scoreboard OAM frame 461, captured $2005 vertical scroll pairs, composite small-crowd-before-large-crowd row order, MMC3-style 1KB R0/R1 background slots, and sprite CHR bank 01/table 0.
Renders the arena third-screen slice at native frame 120 so the fade/foreground scoreboard composition can be inspected without running the windowed app.
Renders the default bank/table-switchable intro layout lab with the local CHR sheet and asset-backed TECMO PRESENTS glyph composition.
Renders the Intro Lab with one seeded local placement record so the builder canvas and record list can be verified.
Renders the Intro Lab after running the C-side Bank 31/table 1 rabbit lookup preset as 8x16 sprite pairs 124-12B.
Renders the Intro Lab after running the C-side visual TECMO logo candidate preset for Bank 31/table 1 tiles 180-193.
Renders the Intro Lab after running the C-side composite candidate preset that combines the rabbit lookup pair model with the TECMO visual tile group.
Runs the native synthetic C051/D861 helper self-test, then renders a read-only schematic of the fixed sprite-staging helper contract without local payload bytes.
Renders the Intro Lab against pattern-table half 1 so tile IDs 100-1FF can be inspected.
Renders the default bank/table-switchable CHR playground for tile ID and 2x2 title glyph verification.
Renders the CHR Playground against pattern-table half 1 so tile IDs 100-1FF can be inspected.
Builds the executable, renders every active screenshot test, and validates PNG dimensions.
Builds the executable, runs all active headless native flow tests, and writes a sanitized local-only report.
Finds a local rebuilt NES ROM and emulator candidate for original intro comparison, then writes a sanitized local-only availability report. Use -Launch manually to open the ROM.
Scans the private local decomp for first original title/menu screen source candidates without committing source bytes.
Resolves the Bank 04 title text path through C711, Bank 06 character mapping, and the four-tile glyph table; writes local-only JSON and a probe PNG.
Writes an ignored local-only JSON draft for user-provided Intro Lab bank/table/tile/canvas picks.
Scans private decomp references for safe intro driver and fixed $C051 helper counts without committing source bytes.
Decodes the local-only Bank 04 rabbit stream selector into the fixed $C051/$D861 sprite-record model without committing decoded payload data.

Decodes the local-only Bank 04 TECMO logo selector into the fixed $C051/$D861 sprite-record model and verifies the $A90F selector 00 hit range across selectors 00-04 without committing decoded payload data.
Decodes the local-only Bank 04 rabbit and TECMO pointer streams, infers A7DB/A90F selector counts, and renders an ignored actual-CHR preview for Intro Lab comparison.
Renders the roster browser to a PNG for screenshot QA.

Renders Play Game at the captured first TECMO PRESENTS background layer using the stable full-bright palette stage. Left/Right in the window steps backward through the debug evidence layers, including the first D861 sprite, rabbit stream, L88E7 selector streams, nametable proof, and coordinate audit.
Renders one captured palette fade stage for the first TECMO PRESENTS background layer. Replace play-fade0 with play-fade1 through play-fade4 to inspect the dark-to-bright transition stages.
Renders a source-backed original title probe before CHR/layout mapping is pixel accurate.
quick_launch_points.expected_screen: Source-backed original title probe rendered from local private data.

quick_launch_points.description: Renders a native CHR-backed diagnostic from local title glyph mapping, local CHR data, native adjacent setup staging counts, fixed-helper aggregate counts, fixed-bank vector counts, palette/PPU probe counts, stream-format coverage, stream effect shape, and aggregate staging coverage; helper bodies and palette queue contents are not yet modeled.
quick_launch_points.expected_screen: Ignored local-only CHR glyph probe with dispatcher, native setup staging diagnostics, fixed-helper summary, fixed-bank vector summary, palette/PPU probe summary, stream-table coverage, stream effect shape, and aggregate staging coverage.

rules: Overlay must be runtime-only and must not dump proprietary source data.
Overlay values should be derived from native runtime state and local debug counters.
Screenshot tests should include one overlay-on frame once implemented.
Overlay screenshots committed to this repo must avoid roster names and extracted game assets.

rules: No per-frame heap allocations in gameplay update/render paths.
Use transient arena for temporary frame work and reset it deliberately.
Report arena high-water marks once debug overlay is implemented.

current_findings: The first Bank07 C counterpart is now a native capability layer, not emulator compatibility: it covers verified fixed-helper behavior for D861 sprite/OAM staging, D2D2 unused-OAM hiding, D700 setup-byte copying, and 8x16 sprite tile-pair mapping.
The D861-style intro sprite staging adapter and 8x16 tile-pair helpers now live in tecmo_intro_stage.c/.h instead of the large tecmo_game.c runtime file.

The boot trace now verifies Bank07 reset jumps to fixed bootstrap $CC30, bootstrap schedules the Bank07 task, that task schedules Bank04 $825D, and Bank04 dispatch index 0 targets $88E8 before flowing into the $8988 dual-stream emitter and fixed $C051/$D861 staging path.

The first-sprite trace follows Bank04 $88E8 through $8988 into fixed $C051/$D861, then writes an ignored local report with the first staged record and first rabbit record that has an onscreen Y position.

The Play Game launcher option now opens on the captured first TECMO PRESENTS background layer, auto-advances to the NBA Properties license screen after 120 native update ticks, then advances to the Bank04 arena-transition trace step after another 120 native update ticks, and keeps Left/Right available for the progressive debug evidence steps.

The NBA Properties license screen has a native render target from the Lua frame-0 capture plus the decoded Bank00 nametable stream: 102 PPU tiles across $208E-$22DA, captured attribute/palette state, local CHR Bank 31, and the same low-tile-to-table-1 mapper used by the TECMO splash.

tools/emu_intro_first_sprite_watch.lua is a FCEUX Lua watcher that logs $058D and $0200-$02FF OAM staging changes to ignored build/emu_intro_first_sprite_watch.ndjson for original-run comparison against the ignored first-sprite trace.

tools/emu_intro_memory_watch.lua is a broader FCEUX Lua watcher for the original run. It now defaults to a conservative crash-safe capture mode: OAM frame diffs for $0200-$02FF, $1200 mirror checks, Bank04 scratch bytes including $20/$21/$57/$58/$88/$8A/$07EB/$07EC, $03A0 queue changes, compact $2006/$2007 nametable/attribute/palette write batches across $2000-$2FFF, and deduped MMC3/PPUCTRL/PPUADDR/palette batches.

tools/emu_intro_arena_irq_watch.lua is a narrow FCEUX Lua watcher for the third arena screen. It resets ignored build/emu_intro_arena_irq_watch.ndjson and .log on each run, waits for R0=$14/R1=$16, then captures a short window of deduped mapper slot changes, IRQ writes, $2005 scroll pairs, compact OAM facts, and small PPU row snapshots.
The native runtime now boots directly into the launcher menu first; the source-backed Title Screen remains reachable from the menu.

The native launcher Title Screen renders the original-run frame 16 nametable capture: 58 non-empty PPU writes across $210B-$21EB, with PRESENTS at $21CF-$21D6 and the low rabbit/TECMO tiles translated through captured MMC3 CHR state R0=$FC/R1=$FA into the local Bank 31 CHR view. Captured frame-17 attribute bytes and the dark-to-bright palette fade stages are now modeled natively.

$C05A/$89DD is now modeled as a palette snapshot path, not a layout stream: fixed $C05A jumps to $D700, copies 16 bytes from Bank04 $89DD-$89EC into the local palette staging area, and the new L88E7 proof render displays those bytes beside the two A7DB streams.

Bank04 L88E7 seeds $88=A8, $8A=3C, $0352=01, $0100=05, $57=08, and $58=09 before calling L8988. The decoded first intro proof shows A7DB selector $00 as a 55-record companion OAM stream and A7DB selector $01 as the 16-record rabbit stream.

The third intro screen is now sourced from the refreshed original-run Lua capture: Bank04 $88E8 loads screen id $18 through the fixed $C003/$D92E/$D9F6 nametable path, frames 428-430 provide the stadium/crowd background across PPU $2000/$2400 pages, frames 425-476 provide the dark-to-bright palette stages, frame 461 provides the live 55-sprite scoreboard OAM layer, and frame 614+ $2005 writes show the arena vertical scroll advancing after the initial wait.

The arena native renderer now resolves background tiles through MMC3-style 1KB R0/R1 CHR slots instead of a single 8KB bank shortcut, applies the captured $0301-style vertical scroll, composes the arena row stream so the small pixel crowd/stairs band appears above the larger foreground crowd/court band, and maps the scoreboard OAM through MMC3 R2/R3=$08/$09 as local CHR bank 01/table 0. Local-only TECMO_ARENA_* overrides exist for CHR slot experiments without committing generated assets.

The targeted arena IRQ watcher run started at original frame 428, completed at frame 788, confirmed the IRQ path writes $C000=$A8 before switching background R0/R1 from $14/$16 to $5E/$60 around frame 462, and showed the $2000 bottom-row nametable data remains stable through the captured window.
The Play Game arena proof now uses the corrected arena row order: blue upper arena, small pixel crowd/stairs, lower light strip, then larger foreground crowd/court as the scroll advances.

$0352/$0100 are now separated from the PRESENTS text search: the fixed NMI tail writes $0352 to mapper registers and uses $0100=05 to select IRQ/vector target $FCF6, while the normal-letter PRESENTS glyph data lead is in the Bank00 C-0191 text/layout stream around bank00:$8549.

The Bank00 title background stream is consumed by the fixed-bank screen/nametable loader, not the A7DB/A90F OAM interpreters: the emulator capture shows frame 16 writing the visible rabbit/TECMO/PRESENTS layer to PPU $210B-$21EB, with PRESENTS at row 14 columns 15-22.

The coordinate audit still records the older split debug evidence for A7DB/A90F OAM streams, but the first visible TECMO PRESENTS screen is now sourced from the PPU nametable capture rather than that cross-state assembly.
The Play Game stepper keeps the old cross-state coordinate assembly preview as a debug step only; the default Play Game view is the captured PPU background layer.

The Bank07 native self-test now includes the decoded L88E7 $CDAC IRQ setup contract: with $0352=01, $0100=05, $05B6 bit 0 set, $0305=0, $0301=32, and $88=A8, the model selects IRQ vector $FCF6 and produces the expected phase latch/address values.
The next original-intro decode target is the fade-out transition and handoff after the captured first TECMO PRESENTS background layer.
The native launcher exposes a Title Screen mode before the Play Game progressive intro-output mode so the title remains reachable from the menu.

The native launcher exposes an Intro Lab builder that renders the selected local-only CHR sheet, lets the user place selected tiles onto a target canvas, shows local placement records, and can save ignored placement JSON.
The native launcher exposes a CHR Playground that renders selected-bank/table tile IDs $080-$0AF or $180-$1AF and assembled title glyphs for number/letter verification.

Intro Lab and CHR Playground default to Bank 31 table 0 because that is the verified title glyph path. Intro Lab uses Q/E for bank, T for table, Tab for source/canvas focus, Space to record, R to add the Bank 31/table 1 rabbit head/upper lookup subset, M to add the visual TECMO 180-193 candidate, C to add the combined intro candidate, and S to save ignored placement JSON. CHR Playground keeps Left/Right/Tab bank switching and Up/Down table switching.
The --flow-test command now exercises menu-first startup, roster browsing, Play Game progressive output entry/cancel, watched RAM mode/menu bytes, and the quit menu path without opening a window.

The current rabbit lookup path is Bank 04 L88E7 -> L8988 -> fixed $C051/$D861 through Bank 0 pointer table $A7DB. The seeded rabbit pass is selector $01, pointer entry $A7DD, stream pointer $A8C0, with 16 records. Its visible head/upper subset includes OAM tile lows $25, $27, $29, and $2B, which imply table-1 8x16 sprite pairs $124/$125, $126/$127, $128/$129, and $12A/$12B.

The intro-c051-d861-model screenshot is gated by a native synthetic helper self-test and renders a read-only schematic of the fixed helper contract so the native model can be reviewed without committing decoded payload rows.
tools/Find-NesReferenceIntro.ps1 detects a local rebuilt NES ROM plus Mesen/FCEUX-style emulator candidates and writes only a sanitized ignored availability report for original intro comparison.
CHR Playground visual inspection points to Bank 31/table 1 tiles $180-$193 for the TECMO logo candidate, matching five 2x2 letters.

The stronger TECMO execution lead is Bank 04 L8818 -> fixed $C051/$D861 using Bank 0 pointer table $A90F selector $00. The local-only mapper infers five $A90F entries, verifies selector $00 has 46 records and ten $80-$93 OAM tile-low hits covering table-1 pairs $180/$181 through $192/$193, and confirms the rabbit $A7DB selectors $00-$03 have zero hits in that range.

tools/Find-IntroCompositeTrace.ps1 now writes ignored build/intro_composite_trace.json and build/intro_composite_trace_preview.png with full local pointer-row evidence for A7DB selector $01 rabbit, A7DB selector $00 partner stream, A90F selector $00 TECMO hits/context, and the L8645 A90F selector $01 follow-on lead.
Each 8KB local CHR bank exposes 512 8x8 tiles: table 0 is tile IDs $000-$0FF and table 1 is tile IDs $100-$1FF.
Bank 04 title loop dispatches A=0x38 through fixed helper C711.
C711 entry 0x38 resolves locally to Bank 06 text setup at 06:$9E50.
Title characters are mapped through the Bank 06 character-to-tile helper, the baseline $A273,X lookup operand, and the 2x2 glyph tile table at 06:$AF05.
The exact Bank 04 BA16 entry sets the $05B6 update flag bit.
Adjacent setup now has a local-only range/call/write/table report and a native setup summary rendered by original-title-chr.
The setup stream format/effect summary verifies 15 stream table entries and five terminated selector rows without including payload bytes.
BAA4 stream records are modeled as one count byte, two base-offset bytes, and four private source fields per record that emit four staged bytes.
The aggregate native staging pass covers 15 selected streams, 415 records, and 1660 staged writes across local staging range $0200-$0287; payload bytes are not retained.
The current TECMO PRESENTS execution-procedure lead is Bank 04 sequence/dispatch setup feeding fixed helper $C051, which resolves locally to $D861 and appears to stage sprite/OAM-style records.
Bank 00 C-0191/C-0192 are the current text/layout data leads for PRESENTS and TECMO, while Bank 00 C-0195 is a likely mascot/composite tile-block lead.
The BAA4 helper range is corrected to 04:$BAA4-$BAF0 so its fixed finalize call is included in native diagnostics.

Fixed helper calls are summarized as five known helper targets, six invocations, two frame-wait calls with aggregate wait request 20, two staging seed calls, one setup finalize call, and one stream finalize call.
The five fixed helper targets used by the title setup path are verified as five fixed-bank jump entries with five resolved targets spanning 0xC419-0xF2F6; helper body decoding remains pending.

The title setup palette/PPU probe checks three Bank 04 title setup ranges and finds zero direct PPU address writes, zero direct PPU data writes, and zero direct palette-address high-byte literals, leaving the fixed-helper/queued PPU path as the next palette decode gate.
rules: Generated source-map reports stay local and ignored under build.
Public docs may list chunk IDs, relative paths, labels, ranges, and unresolved gates only.
Do not commit ASM text, ROM bytes, extracted CHR bytes, generated proprietary data, or screenshots rendered from original assets.

incremental_milestones.title: Clean public tooling repo
incremental_milestones.done_when: Repo builds and contains no ASM, ROM, extracted assets, or generated game data.

incremental_milestones.title: Prototype launcher and roster flow
incremental_milestones.done_when: Native window launches; screenshot tests render Title Screen, Play Game progressive intro-output mode, and Rosters; --flow-test proves rosters, Play Game entry/cancel, and quit transitions.

incremental_milestones.title: Debug overlay and runtime counters
incremental_milestones.done_when: F3 toggles overlay; overlay shows mode, frame, arena use, selected team/player, and watched RAM bytes.

incremental_milestones.title: Automated screenshot test runner
incremental_milestones.done_when: A single command renders all declared screenshot tests and validates dimensions/files.

incremental_milestones.title: Original title/menu render investigation
incremental_milestones.done_when: Local-only tooling identifies CHR/layout sources needed to render the first original menu screen.

incremental_milestones.title: Source-backed original title probe
incremental_milestones.done_when: A quick launch renders a title probe from local private Bank 04 title data.

incremental_milestones.title: Pixel-accurate title CHR and palette pass
incremental_milestones.done_when: The original title quick launch uses mapped CHR tiles, palette state, and layout data instead of the probe font.

next_engineering_step.title: Pixel-accurate title CHR and palette pass
notes: Decode resolved fixed helper bodies into explicit native pattern-table/VRAM staging operations where needed.
Decode the fixed-helper/queued PPU path that initializes palette RAM and any title palette animation state.
Keep extracted/generated CHR, palette, or script caches local and ignored.
Continue committing only safe project UI screenshots; original-title screenshots stay under ignored build output.
